references.text:
[ErPo80] P. Erd\H{o}s and C. Pomerance, Matching the natural numbers up to $n$ with distinct multiples of another interval. Indigationes Math. (1980), 147-151.
 
 [Gu04] Guy, Richard K., Unsolved problems in number theory. (2004), xviii+437.

remarks.text:
A problem of Erd\H{o}s and Pomerance \cite{ErPo80}, who proved that\[h(n) \ll \frac{n^{3/2}}{(\log n)^{1/2}}.\]Erd\H{o}s and Selfridge proved $h(n)>(3-o(1))n$, and Ruzsa proved $h(n)/n\to \infty$. 

This is discussed in problem B32 of Guy's collection \cite{Gu04}.

See also [375].

statement.text:
Let $h(n)$ be such that, for any $m\geq 1$, in the interval $(m,m+h(n))$ there exist distinct integers $a_i$ for $1\leq i\leq \pi(n)$ such that $p_i\mid a_i$, where $p_i$ denotes the $i$th prime. 

Estimate $h(n)$.
